heading: Special security assistance for modernization of Armed Forces of Korea
label: 2428b Special security assistance for modernization of Armed Forces of Korea
notes: References in TextThe Foreign Assistance Act of 1961, referred to in subsec. (a)(2), is , , , which is classified principally to this chapter (§ 2151 et seq.). Chapter 2 of part II of that Act is classified generally to part II (§ 2311 et seq.) of subchapter II of this chapter. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.Pub. L. 87–19575 Stat. 424section 2151 of this titleSept. 4, 1961
CodificationSection was enacted as part of the International Security Assistance Act of 1978, and not as part of the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: Transfer authority of President of defense articles and services located in Korea; applicable terms and conditions; reimbursement
children.label: a Transfer authority of President of defense articles and services located in Korea; applicable terms and conditions; reimbursement
A
children.text: to transfer, without reimbursement, to the Republic of Korea, only in conjunction with the withdrawal of the 2d Infantry Division and support forces from Korea, such United States Government-owned defense articles as he may determine which are located in Korea in the custody of units of the United States Army scheduled to depart from Korea; and

children.label: B
children.text: to furnish to the Republic of Korea, without reimbursement, defense services (including technical and operational training) in Korea directly related to the United States Government-owned defense articles transferred to the Republic of Korea under this subsection.

Any transfer under the authority of this section shall be made in accordance with all the terms and conditions of the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 [ et seq.] applicable to the furnishing of defense articles and defense services under chapter 2 of part II of that Act [ et seq.], except that no funds heretofore or hereafter appropriated under that Act shall be available to reimburse any agency of the United States Government for any such transfer or related services.22 U.S.C. 215122 U.S.C. 2311

children.heading: Additional transfer authority of President of defense articles located outside of Korea; prerequisites for determinations respecting transfers; report by President to Congress of determinations
children.label: b Additional transfer authority of President of defense articles located outside of Korea; prerequisites for determinations respecting transfers; report by President to Congress of determinations

children.text: the transfer of specific defense articles located in Korea would have a significant adverse impact on the readiness of the United States Armed Forces;
the defense capability provided by those defense articles is needed by the Armed Forces of the Republic of Korea in order to maintain the military balance on the Korean peninsula; and

a comparable defense capability could be provided by less advanced defense articles in the stocks of the Department of Defense which could be transferred without significant adverse impact on the readiness of the United States Armed Forces.

children.heading: Report by President to Congress of types, etc., of transferred defense articles
children.label: c Report by President to Congress of types, etc., of transferred defense articles
children.text: The President shall transmit to the Congress, together with the presentation materials for security assistance programs proposed for each fiscal year through and including the fiscal year 1983, a report describing the types, quantities, and value of defense articles furnished or intended to be furnished to the Republic of Korea under this section.

children.heading: Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 97–113, title VII, § 734(a)(12)95 Stat. 1560Dec. 29, 1981
children.label: d Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 97–113, title VII, § 734(a)(12)95 Stat. 1560Dec. 29, 1981

children.heading: Congressional policy respecting further troop withdrawals
children.label: e Congressional policy respecting further troop withdrawals

children.text: It is the sense of the Congress that further withdrawal of ground forces of the United States from the Republic of Korea may seriously risk upsetting the military balance in that region and requires full advance consultation with the Congress.
